description_long: This is a bright red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft such as the Fokker Dr.I. The fuselage is streamlined and painted red, adorned with black and white Iron Cross insignias on the wings and tail, indicating a historical German military design. The nose features a wooden two-blade propeller and a simple, open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both painted red with black and white markings. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric-covered wings and control surfaces.
description: A red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, black and white Iron Cross insignias, a wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear. The open cockpit and historical markings evoke early 20th-century military aircraft.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red fuselage, three stacked wings, wooden propeller, and tail with Iron Cross insignia.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the triple wing configuration and large black and white insignias on the upper wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and wing structure.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the triple wings, propeller, and fixed landing gear with two main wheels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
